chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Crl. Misc. Application No.S-35 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A No.740/2026 2. For orders on M.A No.695/2026 08.04.2026 Mr. Farhan Ahmed Bozdar, advocate a/w applicant. Mr. Zohaib Hassan Mari, advocate for respondent No.1/alleged contemnor. Mr. Dhani Bakhsh Mari, Assistant Prosecutor General Sindh. = Application No.695/2026 (Modification) The respondent No.1 has preferred an application for modification of the order dated 26.02.2026, wherein a visitation schedule was devised for the interim period, whilst disposing of the instant Crl. Misc. Application. Learned counsel for respondent No.1 submits that the schedule devised by this Court in the above-noted order cannot be complied with in letter and spirit. He further contended that this Court has ample power to modify/review its order and to devise a schedule in the best interest of the minor. Conversely, learned counsel for the applicant submits that in compliance with the meeting schedule of the above-noted order, the applicant, being the mother, has been deprived of meeting with the minor and that the best interest of the minor lies with the applicant. He further submits that respondent No.1 is unnecessarily creating hurdles in the meeting schedule in violation of the order dated 26.02.2026. He lastly 2 prayed that respondent No.1 may be directed to comply with the order dated 26.02.2026. I have heard learned counsel for both the parties and perused the record. Learned counsel for respondent No.1 has raised objections pertaining to the above-noted order and, in essence, is seeking review of the same.

I have heard learned counsel for both the parties and perused the record. Learned counsel for respondent No.1 has raised objections pertaining to the above-noted order and, in essence, is seeking review of the same. The argument of learned counsel for respondent No.1 that this Court exceeded its jurisdiction in the above-noted order cannot be entertained in review jurisdiction and learned counsel for respondent No.1 has the liberty to prefer an appeal in accordance with law, if so advised. With these observations, the application stands dismissed. Application No.740/2026 (Contempt Application): Contempt application is disposed of directing the alleged contemnor to comply with the Order dated 26.02.2026 in its letter and spirit. JUDGE *Faisal*
